touches.ai_summary: Brief cold call with Ravi Allen, Head of Sales at NextAnalytics. Ravi expressed interest in improving pipeline visibility for their sales team. Mentioned they're currently evaluating solutions including Gong. Agreed to a qualification call next week.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently facing challenges with sales pipeline visibility?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our team of 18 reps struggles to get a clear view of deal health and forecast accuracy.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Ravi Allen. Discussed NextAnalytics' current pain points around pipeline reporting and deal coaching. Ravi shared that they're a 1000-person marketing agency with limited automation currently. He's evaluating Gong as well but wants to see how our solution handles automation at scale.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need for better pipeline visibility right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've grown quickly and our manual reporting processes can't keep up. Leadership needs real-time insights.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation compare to Gong's?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation tier removes all bottlenecks on call recording and analysis, whereas Gong typically has usage limits.

next_steps: Ravi to share current tech stack
Schedule deeper discovery call

touches.ai_summary: Extended qualification call with Ravi. Walked through NextAnalytics' current sales process and identified key gaps in deal tracking and coaching workflows. Ravi confirmed budget availability around $15-20K annually. He mentioned needing to involve IT and sales management in evaluation but wants to build the business case first.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in this decision?
questions_asked.answer: IT will need to approve from a security standpoint, and our sales managers will want to test it with their teams.

questions_asked.question: What's your implementation timeline typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: We can usually get teams up and running within 2 weeks, with full training support.

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range for this type of solution?
questions_asked.answer: We're looking at solutions in the $15-20K range for our 18-seat team.

next_steps: Send pricing overview and case study
Ravi to review internally and identify evaluation team

touches.ai_summary: Deep-dive qualification call with Ravi. Discussed specific use cases including deal risk scoring, automated call summaries, and pipeline forecasting. Ravi expressed strong interest in the unlimited automation tier. He confirmed he's championing the project internally but noted decision will need buy-in from sales leadership. Gong remains in consideration but pricing is a concern for them.

questions_asked.question: Can your system integrate with our existing CRM and pull historical data?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs and can backfill up to 2 years of historical deal data.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Better forecast accuracy and our managers spending less time on pipeline reviews, more time coaching.

questions_asked.question: How does Gong's pricing compare for our size?
questions_asked.answer: Gong typically runs higher for mid-market teams, especially with usage-based pricing. Our flat unlimited model is more predictable.

objections: We need to make sure this doesn't add more tools our reps have to log into daily
next_steps: Move to discovery phase
Ravi to schedule intro with IT director Michael Lima

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Ravi after moving to discovery. Shared detailed product overview deck and ROI calculator tailored to NextAnalytics' 18-seat team. Ravi responded positively and confirmed he's setting up time with Michael Lima from IT for technical validation.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over security documentation for our IT review?
questions_asked.answer: Attached SOC 2 Type II report and security whitepaper.

next_steps: Coordinate technical eval with Michael Lima
Ravi to share deck with sales managers

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Ravi to map out evaluation process and timeline. Ravi confirmed Michael Lima and Ivy Watson (Sales Manager) will be key stakeholders in the technical evaluation. Discussed rollout plan for 18 seats. Ravi mentioned he's still comparing with Gong but appreciates our pricing transparency. No executive sponsor identified yet for final approval.

questions_asked.question: Who will be the final decision-maker on this purchase?
questions_asked.answer: Ultimately our VP of Sales will need to sign off, but I'm driving the evaluation and recommendation.

questions_asked.question: Will we get a dedicated customer success manager during onboarding?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, all customers get a dedicated CSM through the first 6 months, then transition to pooled support.

next_steps: Schedule technical eval with Michael Lima
Send Ivy Watson demo invite

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Michael Lima (IT Director). Michael was thorough in reviewing security, data governance, and integration architecture. He asked detailed questions about API rate limits, data retention policies, and SSO setup. Generally positive but flagged that he'll need to run this through their standard vendor security review process. No major blockers identified but timeline will depend on IT approval cycle.

questions_asked.question: What's your data encryption approach for data at rest and in transit?
questions_asked.answer: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, with regional data residency options.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle PII and sensitive call content?
questions_asked.answer: Configurable PII redaction, role-based access controls, and audit logging for all data access.

questions_asked.question: What's the process if we need to export all our data?
questions_asked.answer: Self-service export via API or dashboard, or we can provide a full data dump within 48 hours of request.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical security review timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks depending on our queue, but I can prioritize this if there's business urgency.

objections: Our standard vendor review process can take a few weeks - need to confirm we can meet your proposed timeline
next_steps: Michael to initiate formal security review
Share additional compliance documentation
Check in with Michael in one week

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Ravi Allen. Presented formal proposal for 18 seats at $16,200 ACV with unlimited automation tier. Ravi appreciates the pricing but mentioned Gong has come in with a competitive quote. He's advocating internally but noted the VP of Sales hasn't been directly engaged yet and wants to understand the ROI more clearly. Discussed success metrics and implementation timeline.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down the ROI based on our team's current pipeline size?
questions_asked.answer: Based on your $4M pipeline, even a 5% improvement in forecast accuracy saves ~$200K in resource misallocation annually.

questions_asked.question: When do you need to make a final decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally by end of quarter, but if the business case is strong we can move faster.

next_steps: Ravi to present proposal to VP of Sales
Follow up on Michael's security review status
Schedule follow-up call next week

touches.ai_summary: Second proposal call with Ravi. Ravi shared that Ivy Watson (Sales Manager) is supportive after seeing the demo, but the VP of Sales hasn't reviewed the proposal yet. Gong is still in active consideration and Ravi mentioned they've offered a discount to match our pricing. Ravi is pushing to get VP of Sales engaged but there's no confirmed meeting scheduled yet. Michael Lima's security review is still in progress.

questions_asked.question: What would help get the VP of Sales more engaged at this stage?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, a customer reference in the marketing agency space would be really valuable.

questions_asked.question: If Gong matches your pricing, what differentiates your solution?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation means no overage fees as you scale usage, plus faster implementation and more flexible integrations.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything blocking us from moving forward besides the executive approval?
questions_asked.answer: Just need Michael's final security sign-off and the VP meeting, then we should be good to proceed.

objections: We need to see a strong differentiation from Gong if pricing is similar
next_steps: Provide marketing agency customer reference
Ravi to schedule meeting with VP of Sales
Check in on security review

touches.ai_summary: Pricing and negotiation call with Ravi. Ravi asked if there's flexibility on pricing given Gong's competitive offer. Discussed potential discount scenarios and contract terms. Ravi confirmed Michael Lima cleared security review with no blockers. Still waiting on VP of Sales meeting - Ravi mentioned the VP is traveling and availability is limited. Ravi remains positive but acknowledged timeline is slipping.

questions_asked.question: Is there any room to come down on the $16,200 ACV?
questions_asked.answer: We have some flexibility for multi-year commitments or if we can close this quarter.

questions_asked.question: What's the VP's biggest concern at this point?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly just bandwidth - they're evaluating multiple vendor decisions right now. Not a concern with our solution specifically.

next_steps: Send updated proposal with quarterly discount option
Ravi to confirm VP meeting by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Ravi checking in on VP of Sales meeting and next steps. Ravi responded that the VP meeting is scheduled for next week. He's preparing the business case and reference materials. Expressed continued interest but noted timeline is now pushed to next month. Gong is still in consideration.
next_steps: Support Ravi's prep for VP meeting
Send executive summary deck
Follow up after VP meeting

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Ravi Allen and the VP of Sales (attending by phone). Discussed strategic value of pipeline visibility and automation for NextAnalytics' growth plans. VP was engaged but non-committal, asking mostly high-level questions about ROI and vendor stability. Ravi advocated well for the solution. VP asked for time to review with finance and compare final Gong proposal. No clear timeline for decision provided. VP thanked us for the thorough process.

questions_asked.question: How many customers do you have in the mid-market agency space?
questions_asked.answer: About 40 customers in marketing and creative agencies, ranging from 500-2000 employees.

questions_asked.question: What's your company's growth trajectory and funding status?
questions_asked.answer: We're Series B funded, growing 150% YoY with strong retention metrics and a clear path to profitability.

questions_asked.question: What's your decision timeline from here?
questions_asked.answer: We'll need a week or two to finalize our evaluation and get finance aligned. I'll have Ravi follow up with next steps.

next_steps: VP to review with finance
Ravi to coordinate final decision meeting
Follow up in one week
